hypothesis: If a large language model undergoes continual pre-training on domain-specific chemical and MOF literature prior to supervised fine-tuning on MOF property datasets, then it will achieve lower prediction error (MAE) for MOF properties such as band gap and gas adsorption compared to models without domain-specific pre-training and to traditional structure-based baselines (CGCNN, SOAP, MOFormer), because the pre-training phase instills chemical domain knowledge that enables more effective learning from MOFid text representations, particularly in low-resource fine-tuning scenarios.
